reasoning: According to multiple reliable polls conducted up to October 26, 2025 (less than three weeks before the November 16 election), Johannes Kaiser is consistently polling in third or fourth place with roughly 12% to 14% of the vote. The frontrunner, Jeannette Jara, leads with 24% to 31%, followed by José Antonio Kast at 22% to 25%. While Kaiser has seen a moderate upward trend recently, overcoming a double-digit deficit to two separate frontrunners in less than three weeks is exceedingly rare in heavily polled, structured national elections. Thus, the probability of him securing the greatest number of valid votes in the first round is at the lowest bound of reasonable probability (2%).
